question.text: In Options on Futures, devolvement typically happens:

answer.text: 10-13 days before the expiry of the underlying futures contract
On the same day as futures expiry
Only on the last trading day of the year

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Options on Futures devolve around 10-13 days before futures expiry, before the delivery/staggered margin period on futures begins (which typically starts 5-15 calendar days before futures expiry). **The Trap:** It's not on futures expiry day — options expire BEFORE futures to allow time for the devolved futures position management. **Cross-Exam Context:** Devolvement timing is specific to commodity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Traders must know the devolvement date — after options devolve into futures, regular futures margins (including delivery period margins) apply, significantly increasing capital requirements.
